name: chasm-crossing
summary: A bottomless cleft spanned by a precarious crossing — rope-and-plank bridge, or a natural stone arch, with ledges on either rim.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: long — the chasm cuts the long axis

primary_palette.note: swaying plank bridge over the drop
natural rock span — narrower than it looks
lowered cage or basket to reach the far side
the bridge is gone — party must improvise

story_prompts: something is already on the bridge — you both meet in the middle
the ropes look new, the bridge is bait, something waits below
a flying foe harries the crossing — every turn on the bridge is a save
the far ledge is occupied, and they're already cutting the ropes

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: very dim (0.1), lanterns on each rim only

anti_patterns: the chasm must read as deadly — don't make it narrow enough to simply step over
ledges should be shallow, forcing the party toward the crossing
don't decorate the bridge itself — clean span, threat is traversal
